query: Amazon River is approximately 6400 km long
answer: The Amazon River is generally believed to be at least 6,400 km long, making it the second longest river in the world after the Nile. Most scientists agree on its length being around 4,345 miles (6,992 km). It is also famous for the rainforest along its shores.

results.title: How Long Is the Amazon River? | Britannica
results.snippet: It is the largest river by volume, and its basin is home to the Amazon Rainforest, the world’s richest and most-varied biological reservoir. Most scientists believe the South American river is at least 4,000 miles (6,400 km) long—still shorter than the Nile, which is widely held to be the world’s longest river at about 4,132 miles (6,650 km). In fact, even the definition of what constitutes a source has been debated—though most scientists agree it is the farthest point of continuously flowing water that can travel to the river’s mouth. Concerning its end point, the Amazon has three main outlets to the Atlantic Ocean: two on the northern side of Marajó Island in Brazil and one to the island’s south that joins the Pará River. They determined that Apacheta Creek—which is 6 miles (10 km) longer than the nearby Carhuasanta—is actually the Amazon’s source. In the end, the Amazon River’s total length was found to be 4,345 miles (6,992 km).

results.snippet: The Amazon River is 4,000 miles (6,400 km) long. Despite the debate about the accuracy of the length that is still a remarkable distance.
